needs.social.hi: It is a friendly hello when you see a person.
needs.social.bye: You say 'bye' when you leave or someone goes.
It is how you say goodbye.
needs.social.sorry: You say 'sorry' when you feel bad about something.
It tells someone you did not mean to hurt or upset them.
You can say 'sorry' and give a gentle hug.
needs.social.love_you: 'I love you' tells someone you care about them so much.
You say it to family and people close to your heart.
You can say 'I love you' and give a big hug.
needs.social.hug: A hug is wrapping your arms around someone you love.
You squeeze gently to feel close and warm.
You can ask for a hug when you need one.
needs.gestalts.good_morning: You say 'good morning' to greet someone when you wake up.
It is a cheerful hello at the start of the day.
You can say 'good morning!' to your family at breakfast.
needs.gestalts.good_night: You say 'good night' before you go to sleep.
It is how you say goodbye at bedtime.
You can say 'good night' and get a goodnight kiss.
needs.gestalts.have_a_good_day: You say 'have a good day' to wish someone a nice day.
It is a kind goodbye in the morning.
You can say it when someone leaves for the day.
needs.gestalts.see_you_later: You say 'see you later' when you part for a while.
It means goodbye, and you will meet again soon.
You can say 'see you later!' with a wave.
needs.gestalts.lets_go: You say 'let's go' when you are ready to leave or start.
It means come on, it is time to move.
You can say 'let's go!' when you are excited.
needs.gestalts.ready_to_play: You say 'ready to play' when you want to start playing.
It tells a friend you are set to have fun.
You can say 'I'm ready to play!'
needs.gestalts.i_want_that: 'I want that' tells someone what you would like.
You point and say it to ask for something.
You can say 'I want that' to get your snack or toy.
needs.gestalts.i_need_help: 'I need help' tells someone you cannot do it alone.
You say it when something is too hard.
You can say 'I need help!' and someone will come.
needs.gestalts.more_please: You say it when you want another, with kind manners.
You can say 'more please' for more snack or play.
needs.gestalts.all_done_eat: 'All done eating' tells someone your meal is finished.
You say it when your tummy is full.
You can say 'all done!' to leave the table.
needs.gestalts.where_are_you: 'Where are you?' asks where someone is.
You say it when you cannot find a person.
You can call out 'where are you?' to find someone.
needs.gestalts.whats_that: 'What's that?' asks about something you see.
You say it when you are curious about a thing.
You can point and say 'what's that?'
needs.gestalts.i_love_you: 'I love you' tells someone how much you care.
You say it to the people closest to your heart.
You can say 'I love you' and snuggle close.
needs.gestalts.yes_please: 'Yes please' is a polite way to say yes.
You say it when you want something, with good manners.
You can say 'yes please!' when you would like something.
needs.gestalts.no_thank_you: 'No thank you' is a polite way to say no.
You say it to turn something down kindly.
You can say 'no thank you' when you do not want it.
needs.gestalts.excuse_me: You say 'excuse me' to get attention politely or to pass by.
It is a kind way to interrupt or move past.
You can say 'excuse me' to squeeze through.
needs.gestalts.wait_a_minute: 'Wait a minute' asks someone to hold on a little.
You say it when you need a short moment.
You can say 'wait a minute, please!'
needs.gestalts.sorry_about_that: 'Sorry about that' says you feel bad for a little mistake.
You say it when something goes wrong by accident.
You can say 'sorry about that' and help fix it.
needs.gestalts.i_see_you: 'I see you' tells someone you spotted them.
You say it in peekaboo or when you find them.
You can giggle and say 'I see you!'
needs.gestalts.time_to: 'Time to' means it is the moment to do something.
You say it before an activity, like 'time to eat.'
You can say 'time to play!' or 'time to go.'
needs.gestalts.happy_birthday: 'Happy birthday' is what you say on someone's special day.
You say it to celebrate the day they were born.
You can sing 'happy birthday' over the cake.
needs.gestalts.i_miss_you: 'I miss you' tells someone you wish they were here.
You say it when you are apart from someone you love.
You can say 'I miss you' on the phone.
needs.social.family.family_hug: A family hug is everyone hugging together.
You all wrap your arms around each other.
You can say 'family hug!' to gather everyone close.
needs.social.family.kiss_affection: A goodnight kiss is a little kiss at bedtime.
It is a soft, loving press before you sleep.
You can get a goodnight kiss when you are tucked in.
needs.social.family.snuggle: To snuggle is to cuddle up close and cozy.
You nestle in someone's arms, warm and safe.
You can snuggle on the couch or at bedtime.
needs.social.family.cuddle: To cuddle is to hold each other close and soft.
You curl up together, warm and gentle.
You can cuddle with a parent or a stuffed animal.
needs.social.family.tickle: A tickle is a light touch that makes you giggle.
Wiggly fingers on your tummy make you laugh.
You can say 'tickle me!' for some giggles.
needs.social.family.piggyback: A piggyback is a ride on someone's back.
You hold on while they carry you along.
You can ask for a piggyback ride.
needs.gestalts.family.carry_me: 'Carry me' asks someone to pick you up and hold you.
You say it when you want to be carried.
You can lift your arms and say 'carry me!'
needs.gestalts.family.hold_me: 'Hold me' asks someone to hold you close.
You say it when you want to be held and safe.
You can say 'hold me' for a cuddle.
needs.gestalts.family.one_more_book: 'One more book' asks to read another story.
You say it at bedtime to keep reading.
You can say 'one more book, please!'
needs.gestalts.family.snuggle_with_me: 'Snuggle with me' asks someone to cuddle up.
You say it when you want to be cozy together.
You can say 'snuggle with me!' on the couch.
needs.gestalts.family.tuck_me_in: 'Tuck me in' asks for cozy covers at bedtime.
You say it so a grown-up pulls up your blanket.
You can say 'tuck me in' before you sleep.
needs.gestalts.family.read_to_me: 'Read to me' asks someone to read a story.
You say it when you want to hear a book.
You can bring a book and say 'read to me!'
needs.gestalts.daily.im_hungry: 'I'm hungry' tells someone your tummy wants food.
You say it when you feel hungry.
You can say 'I'm hungry' to ask for a snack.
needs.gestalts.daily.im_thirsty: 'I'm thirsty' tells someone you want a drink.
You say it when your mouth feels dry.
You can say 'I'm thirsty' to ask for water.
needs.gestalts.daily.i_did_it: 'I did it!' shows you finished something on your own.
You say it when you are proud of what you did.
needs.gestalts.daily.i_need_a_break: 'I need a break' tells someone you want to pause.
You say it when something is too much for now.
You can say 'I need a break' and rest.
needs.gestalts.daily.try_again: 'Try again' means give it another go.
You say it when something did not work the first time.
You can say 'try again!' and keep going.
needs.gestalts.daily.good_job: 'Good job' cheers someone for doing well.
You say it to encourage a friend.
You can say 'good job!' with a thumbs up.
needs.gestalts.daily.my_turn: 'My turn now' tells someone it is your time.
You say it when you are taking your turn.
You can say 'my turn now, please.'
needs.gestalts.daily.your_turn: 'Your turn' tells someone it is their time to go.
You say it to share and take turns.
You can hand over the toy and say 'your turn!'
needs.social.school.raise_hand: You raise your hand to ask or answer in class.
You lift your hand up and wait to be called on.
You raise your hand instead of calling out.
needs.social.school.may_i: 'May I' is a polite way to ask permission.
You say it to ask nicely if you can do something.
You can say 'may I have a turn?'
needs.social.school.line_leader: The line leader gets to be first in line.
You stand at the front and lead the way.
It is special to be the line leader.
needs.social.school.quiet_please: 'Quiet please' asks everyone to be calm and soft.
You say it when it is time to listen.
You can put a finger to your lips and say 'quiet please.'
needs.social.school.my_turn_share: Sharing time is when you take turns with things.
Everyone gets a turn to share.
It is kind to share during sharing time.
needs.social.school.circle_time: Circle time is when the class sits in a circle together.
You listen to stories and songs as a group.
You sit criss-cross during circle time.
needs.social.school.centers_time: Centers are fun activity stations in the classroom.
You move to different spots to play and learn.
You choose a center to explore.
needs.time.now: 'Now' means at this very moment — right away.
It is happening this second, not later.
You can say 'I want it now!'
needs.time.later: 'Later' means after a while, not right now.
It will happen soon, but not yet.
A grown-up might say 'we'll do it later.'
needs.time.soon: 'Soon' means in a little while — coming up fast.
It is not right now, but not far away either.
needs.time.today: It is the day that is happening right now.
needs.time.tomorrow: 'Tomorrow' means the next day, after you sleep tonight.
needs.time.yesterday: It already happened and is in the past.
needs.time.morning: 'Morning' is the early part of the day when you wake up.
The sun comes up and you eat breakfast.
You say 'good morning' in the morning.
needs.time.afternoon: 'Afternoon' is the middle of the day, after lunch.
It comes after morning and before evening.
You might play outside in the afternoon.
needs.time.night: 'Night' is the dark time when you sleep.
The moon and stars come out at night.
needs.time.before: 'Before' means it happens first, earlier than something else. The opposite is after.
You wash your hands before you eat.
needs.time.after: 'After' means it happens next, later than something else. The opposite is before.
You brush your teeth after dinner.
needs.time.hours.hour_1: One o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 1, and the big hand points up to the 12.
Something might happen at one o'clock, like a nap.
needs.time.hours.hour_2: Two o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 2, and the big hand points to the 12.
You might play at two o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_3: Three o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 3, and the big hand points to the 12.
Snack might be at three o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_4: Four o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 4, and the big hand points to the 12.
You might play outside at four o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_5: Five o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 5, and the big hand points to the 12.
Dinner might start around five o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_6: Six o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 6, and the big hand points to the 12.
You might eat dinner at six o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_7: Seven o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 7, and the big hand points to the 12.
Bath time might be at seven o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_8: Eight o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 8, and the big hand points to the 12.
Bedtime might be around eight o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_9: Nine o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 9, and the big hand points to the 12.
In the morning, nine o'clock is playtime.
needs.time.hours.hour_10: Ten o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 10, and the big hand points to the 12.
Snack might be at ten o'clock in the morning.
needs.time.hours.hour_11: Eleven o'clock is a time on the clock.
The little hand points to the 11, and the big hand points to the 12.
Lunch is coming soon at eleven o'clock.
needs.time.hours.hour_12: Twelve o'clock is a time on the clock.
Both hands point straight up to the 12.
Twelve o'clock in the day is lunchtime.
needs.time.clock.noon: Noon is the middle of the day — twelve o'clock in the daytime.
The sun is high up in the sky at noon.
You usually eat lunch around noon.
needs.time.clock.midnight: Midnight is the middle of the night — twelve o'clock when it is dark.
Everyone is fast asleep at midnight.
Midnight is the start of a brand-new day.
needs.time.clock.oclock: 'O'clock' tells what hour it is on the clock.
You say it when the big hand points to the 12, like 'three o'clock.'
You can ask 'what o'clock is it?'
needs.time.clock.half_past: 'Half past' means thirty minutes after the hour.
The big hand points all the way down to the 6.
Half past two is in between two and three o'clock.
needs.time.clock.quarter: A 'quarter' is fifteen minutes — a quarter of an hour.
The big hand moves a quarter of the way around.
'Quarter past' is fifteen minutes after the hour.
needs.time.clock.minute: A minute is a small amount of time.
You can wait a minute for something.
needs.time.clock.hour: An hour is a longer amount of time.
A nap might last about an hour.
needs.time.months.january: January is the first month of the year.
It comes after December and starts the new year.
January is often cold and snowy.
needs.time.months.february: February is the second month of the year.
It is the shortest month, after January.
needs.time.months.march: March is the third month of the year.
It comes after February, when spring begins.
needs.time.months.april: April is the fourth month of the year.
It comes after March, in spring.
needs.time.months.may: May is the fifth month of the year.
It comes after April, in spring.
needs.time.months.june: June is the sixth month of the year.
It comes after May, when summer begins.
needs.time.months.july: July is the seventh month of the year.
It comes after June, in the summer.
needs.time.months.august: August is the eighth month of the year.
It comes after July, in the summer.
needs.time.months.september: September is the ninth month of the year.
It comes after August, when fall begins.
needs.time.months.october: October is the tenth month of the year.
It comes after September, in the fall.
needs.time.months.november: November is the eleventh month of the year.
It comes after October, in the fall.
needs.time.months.december: December is the twelfth and last month of the year.
It comes after November, when winter begins.
December is cold and has the holidays.
needs.time.seasons.spring: Spring is the season when flowers bloom and it warms up.
Baby animals are born and trees grow new leaves.
It rains in spring to help things grow.
needs.time.seasons.summer: Summer is the warm, sunny season.
The days are long and hot.
You swim and play outside in summer.
needs.time.seasons.winter: It can snow, and the days are short.
You bundle up warm in winter.
needs.time.units.second: A second is a tiny amount of time.
It is very quick — you can clap once in a second.
needs.time.units.week: A week is seven days in a row.
It goes from Sunday all the way to Saturday.
There are about four weeks in a month.
needs.time.units.year: A year is a long time — twelve whole months.
Your birthday comes once every year.
You get one year older each year.
needs.time.units.weekend: The weekend is Saturday and Sunday.
It is the days when there is no school.
You relax and play on the weekend.
needs.time.units.weekday: A weekday is a day from Monday to Friday.
These are the school and work days.
You go to school on a weekday.
needs.time.units.tonight: 'Tonight' means this very night, coming up.
It is the night part of today.
